items.headline: Wall Street Retreats as Inflation Fears and Tech Selloff Weigh
items.summary: U.S. stocks saw a broad retreat on Tuesday, with major indices closing lower as hotter-than-expected inflation data and rising oil prices fueled investor concerns. A notable selloff in artificial intelligence-linked technology shares further cooled the market's recent record-setting rally. Geopolitical tensions, particularly surrounding the prolonged Iran conflict, also contributed to a cautious sentiment among investors. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Fell 0.6 percent amid inflation fears and tech selloff.
Declined 0.9 percent, weighed down by AI-linked stocks.

items.headline: Quantum Computing surges on strong Q1 results
items.summary: Shares jumped after reporting Q1 2026 results that featured a massive year-over-year revenue increase and a narrower-than-expected loss, driven by acquisitions of Luminar Semiconductors and NuCrypt.

items.headline: Sea Limited gains on robust Q1 revenue growth
items.summary: Shares advanced after delivering strong Q1 revenue growth that topped expectations, driven by robust momentum across its e-commerce and fintech businesses.

items.headline: Plug Power up after topping Q1 expectations
items.summary: Shares surged after reporting Q1 results that topped expectations, with top-line growth driven by strength in its material handling and electrolyzer businesses. Management reiterated its FY2026 outlook.

items.headline: Intel falls amid AI-linked stock selloff
items.summary: Intel Corporation shares fell as chipmakers and AI-focused companies led declines after a blistering rally earlier this year.

items.headline: Micron Technology declines with broader tech weakness
items.summary: Micron Technology dropped as the technology sector experienced a selloff, particularly in AI-linked stocks.

items.headline: Power Solutions International plunges
items.summary: Power Solutions International, Inc. saw a significant decline today, marking it as one of the biggest percentage losers.

items.headline: April CPI Report Reveals Hotter-Than-Expected Inflation
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for April 2026 showed a 0.6% increase month-over-month and a 3.8% rise year-over-year, exceeding expectations. Core CPI, excluding volatile food and energy, also increased by 0.4% month-over-month and 2.8% year-over-year, indicating inflation remains well above the Federal Reserve's 2% target. Energy prices, particularly gasoline, contributed significantly to the surge.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Federal Reserve Governor
items.summary: Kevin Warsh was confirmed as a Federal Reserve governor today and is expected to be confirmed as Fed Chair, succeeding Jerome Powell, as early as Wednesday. This leadership transition comes as the market assesses the Fed's future monetary policy in light of persistent inflation.

items.headline: Technology Leads Market Downturn; Energy Sector Shows Resilience
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly AI-linked stocks, led the market lower today. In contrast, the Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) advanced 2.6% on Monday, with Materials ($XLB) and Industrials ($XLI) also showing gains, signaling potential sector rotation. Rising oil prices, influenced by Middle East tensions, benefited energy companies.

items.headline: Zebra Technologies Reports Strong Q1 2026 Results
items.summary: Zebra Technologies Corporation ($ZBRA) announced robust first-quarter 2026 results, with net sales increasing 14.3% year-over-year to $1,495 million. Non-GAAP diluted EPS saw a year-over-year increase to $4.75.

items.headline: Venture Global Delivers Strong Q1 2026 Performance
items.summary: Venture Global, Inc. ($VG) reported strong Q1 2026 financial results, with revenue reaching $4.6 billion, a 59% increase from Q1 2025. Net income also rose by 23% to $488 million.
